overview: From 2026-05-20 to 2026-05-21, elizaos/eliza had 51 new PRs (31 merged), 2 new issues, and 11 active contributors.

topIssues.title: Release orchestrator startup_failure: missing platform-signing secrets
topIssues.author: lalalune
topIssues.body:
## Summary

Two release-orchestration workflows hit `startup_failure` on every tag push (most recently on `v2.0.3`):

- [Release All Platforms](https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26156834072) — `.github/workflows/release-all.yml`, triggered by `push: tags: [v*.*.*]`
- [elizaOS Full OS Release](https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26156833438) — `.github/workflows/elizaos-os-full-release.yml`, triggered by `release: types: [created]`

Both fail before any job runs (`This run likely failed because of a workflow file issue.`). YAML parses cleanly locally; all referenced reusable workflows exist at the tag.

## Root cause

Both orchestrators call reusable workflows whose `secrets:` blocks declare `required: true` for repo secrets that don't exist in `elizaOS/eliza`. When `secrets: inherit` can't supply a `required: true` secret, GitHub fails the parent at startup.

Confirmed via `gh secret list -R elizaOS/eliza` that these are missing:

| Secret | Required by | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `DEBIAN_GPG_PRIVATE_KEY` | `publish-apt-repo.yml` | sign apt repo |
| `DEBIAN_GPG_KEY_ID` | `publish-apt-repo.yml` | sign apt repo |
| `DEBIAN_GPG_PASSPHRASE` | `publish-apt-repo.yml` | sign apt repo |
| `CSC_LINK` | `release-electrobun.yml` (macOS sign) | Developer ID .p12 (base64) |
| `CSC_KEY_PASSWORD` | `release-electrobun.yml` | .p12 password |
| `APPLE_ID` | `release-electrobun.yml`, `apple-store-release.yml` | notarization Apple ID |
| `APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD` | `release-electrobun.yml`, `apple-store-release.yml` | notarization |
| `APPLE_TEAM_ID` | `release-electrobun.yml`, `apple-store-release.yml` | Apple Developer Team ID |
| `ITC_TEAM_ID` | `apple-store-release.yml` | App Store Connect team ID |
| `APP_STORE_APP_ID` | `apple-store-release.yml` | numeric app ID |
| `MATCH_PASSWORD` | `apple-store-release.yml` | Fastlane Match passphrase |
| `MATCH_GIT_URL` | `apple-store-release.yml` | Fastlane Match repo |
| `WINDOWS_SIGN_CERT_BASE64` | `windows-store-release.yml` | Windows code-signing cert |
| `WINDOWS_SIGN_CERT_PASSWORD` | `windows-store-release.yml` | cert password |
| `WINDOWS_SIGN_TIMESTAMP_URL` | `windows-store-release.yml` | RFC 3161 timestamp server |
| `FLATHUB_TOKEN` | `flatpak-publish.yml` | declared `required: false`, not blocking |

The full secret list expected is documented in the header comment of `.github/workflows/release-all.yml`.

## Why the other release workflows worked

`NPM Release`, `Build Agent Image`, `Build elizaOS Linux ISO`, `Build elizaOS Debian Package` (build-only, not publish), `Release AOSP Flasher`, `Release Orchestrator`, `Snap Build & Test`, `Build & Release (Electrobun)`, `Release USB Installer` all started fine — their workflows don't `require: true` any missing secret. (USB Installer separately failed mid-run on a now-fixed `electrobun` PATH issue.)

## Resolution options

1. **Add the missing secrets** to `elizaOS/eliza` → Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions. This is the intended end state per the workflow header docs.
2. **Downgrade `required: true` → `required: false`** in the reusable workflows for platforms we don't intend to ship yet (e.g. if Windows Store / Apple Store / signed apt repo aren't in scope for v2.0.x). The leaf workflows already self-guard most calls.
3. **Stop calling unconfigured publish workflows** from `release-all.yml` until their secrets exist.

Probably want a mix: add Debian GPG (lowest-risk, internal-only) and Apple signing (highest-value), and gate Windows Store + Flathub behind explicit opt-in until those credentials are arranged.

## Context

- Tag for `v2.0.3` is live on `main` (`fa240156ed`). All other release workflows (NPM, Linux ISO, Android, Snap, Electrobun, etc.) ran or are running normally.
- Failed runs are also reproducible on the prior `v2.0.2` attempt — this is the pre-existing block, not a new regression.
- I don't have admin rights to download the run logs, which would confirm the exact secret-validation error message. Anyone with admin can drop the snippet here for the record.

topIssues.title: Benchmarks: enable publishable Hyperliquid live harness runs
topIssues.author: lalalune
topIssues.body:
## Problem

`hyperliquid_bench` is currently gated out of the real eliza/hermes/openclaw benchmark matrix because live execution requires `HL_PRIVATE_KEY` and `--no-demo`. Demo-mode output is intentionally rejected by the scorer and must not be published as a real harness score.

## Current evidence

`python -m benchmarks.orchestrator.cli validate-runtime-gates --json` reports `hyperliquid_live` blocked with: Hyperliquid live execution unavailable (set `HL_PRIVATE_KEY` and run with `--no-demo`).

The scorer rejects demo-mode artifacts and requires scenario-level live execution evidence with confirmed action signatures.

## Acceptance criteria

- Provide a funded/testnet-safe `HL_PRIVATE_KEY` path in benchmark CI or documented local benchmark environment.
- Run `hyperliquid_bench` with `eliza`, `hermes`, and `openclaw` using `--no-demo`.
- Publish only non-demo rows with scenario-level live evidence and unique signatures.
- `validate-runtime-gates` no longer blocks `hyperliquid_live` in the configured live environment.
- Latest publishability/comparability include Hyperliquid without demo/sample/stub fallback.

topPRs.title: chore(release): develop → main (v2.0.2)
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
## Summary

Promote develop to main for the v2.0.2 release. 560 commits behind main, includes:

- elizaOS app + agent runtime updates (RemotePluginTrustPolicy, ViewState widening, evaluator note redaction)
- Cloud platform fixes (mock isolation, db/schemas export integrity, bearer enforcement, Stripe markup/credits)
- iOS native llama xcframework hardening (clean build, lock reuse, opt-in build, timeout bound)
- Discord/orchestrator/UI fixes (reply-reference double-fetch + injection vector, OpenCode endpoint detection, Tailwind v4 z-index)
- Benchmarks + examples build stability (clone-ur-crush atomic manifest writes, isolated build output, nested-install hardening)
- Multiple test stabilizations (cloud mock e2e stack, biome formatting, lint cleanup)

Merging this PR triggers tag push v2.0.2 → release-all.yml (full cross-platform release).

## Test plan

- [x] All required workflows green on develop HEAD (gate: \`Develop Staging Beta Release\` must pass)
- [ ] PR checks green
- [ ] Merge with ff-only after CI passes
- [ ] Push tag v2.0.2 to trigger release-all.yml

topPRs.title: [WIP] First-party Satellites and dynamic view host bridge
topPRs.author: Dexploarer
topPRs.body:
## WIP Scope

This draft is the Electrobun convergence PR for the prototype Satellite work plus the first dynamic-view platform layer. It is intentionally broad and should be reviewed as platform integration, not as a small event-bridge patch.

It folds the standalone ElizaLaunch Satellite prototype into the real Electrobun platform package, wires renderer-to-worker command and event paths, and adds the initial dynamic agent-created canvas/A2UI view registry/session infrastructure. The production app UI remains untouched.

## Major Changes

### First-party Satellites moved into Electrobun

Adds first-party Satellites under:

`packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun/satellites/`

Included Satellites:

- `eliza.runtime`
- `eliza.surface`
- `eliza.fs`
- `eliza.pty`
- `eliza.git`
- `eliza.local-model`

Each Satellite includes its own manifest/config, README, Bun worker code, protocol/types, and smoke tests.

### First-party Satellite seeding

Adds:

- `packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun/src/first-party-satellites.ts`
- `packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun/src/first-party-satellites.test.ts`

The registry marks:

- `eliza.runtime` as required
- `eliza.fs`, `eliza.pty`, `eliza.git`, and `eliza.local-model` as recommended
- `eliza.surface` as dev/admin only

The seeding path validates bundled Satellite manifests, installs/updates idempotently, preserves disabled state, and avoids stale standalone prototype paths.

### Runtime Satellite host-mode adapter

`eliza.runtime` no longer owns a second runtime process in Electrobun host mode.

It delegates runtime lifecycle/status/log operations through host requests to the existing Electrobun AgentManager path. Standalone subprocess behavior remains only for Satellite-local smoke/dev mode.

### Renderer-to-worker command bridge

Adds/uses the renderer-facing command path:

- `carrotInvokeWorker`

This lets dev/admin views and future dynamic views call `eliza.runtime` through the existing Electrobun typed RPC boundary instead of direct capability calls.

### Worker event tail bridge

Adds the renderer-facing event path:

- `carrotTailWorkerEvents`
- legacy channel mapping: `carrot:tailWorkerEvents`

Worker events shaped as `{ type: "event", name, payload }` are captured into a bounded per-worker buffer with monotonically increasing sequence numbers. Renderers can tail events by worker/Satellite ID and sequence cursor.

Defaults:

- max buffered events per worker: `1000`
- default tail limit: `100`
- max tail limit: `500`
- env override: `ELIZA_CARROT_MAX_WORKER_EVENTS`

This is the event substrate for dynamic agent-created views. It is not a live subscription system yet.

### Dynamic agent-created view infrastructure

Adds a host-side dynamic view layer under:

`packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun/src/dynamic-views/`

Included pieces:

- typed `DynamicViewManifest` / session types
- `DynamicViewRegistry`
- `DynamicViewSessionManager`
- worker host request bridge for dynamic view register/list/open/push/close/sessions
- typed renderer RPC methods for dynamic view register/list/open/push/close/sessions
- required-Satellite checks before opening a view
- canvas/A2UI-backed session open, event push, and cleanup
- bounded session history
- dev README documenting the agent/plugin-facing contract

New typed renderer RPC methods:

- `dynamicViewRegister`
- `dynamicViewUnregister`
- `dynamicViewList`
- `dynamicViewOpen`
- `dynamicViewClose`
- `dynamicViewPush`
- `dynamicViewSessions`

New trusted worker host request methods:

- `dynamic-view-register`
- `dynamic-view-unregister`
- `dynamic-view-list`
- `dynamic-view-open`
- `dynamic-view-close`
- `dynamic-view-push`
- `dynamic-view-sessions`

Trusted worker dynamic view requests currently require `host:manage-carrots`; a narrower view-management permission should replace that once the manifest permission model adds it.

### Demo dynamic view

Adds a dev-only demo view:

- view id: `agent.run.trace.demo`
- file: `packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun/src/dynamic-views/demo/agent-run-trace.html`

It demonstrates:

- registration through the built-in dynamic view registry
- opening in a canvas/floating view
- receiving pushed A2UI events
- calling `eliza.runtime` through `carrotInvokeWorker`
- tailing `eliza.runtime` events through `carrotTailWorkerEvents`

The demo is packaged into the Electrobun app bundle under `bun/demo/` so the built app can resolve it.

### Surface remains dev/admin only

`eliza.surface` remains an inspector/control harness.

The Surface Runtime client prefers:

1. `carrotInvokeWorker` for method calls
2. `carrotTailWorkerEvents` for events
3. existing bridge fallback
4. polling only as last fallback

Surface still calls `eliza.runtime` as broker and does not call capability Satellites directly.

### Capability Satellite summaries

`eliza.fs`

- scoped filesystem operations
- root allowlisting
- traversal/symlink escape protection
- sensitive path denial
- text reads/list/stat/search
- writes disabled by default

`eliza.pty`

- trusted local terminal capability
- Bun terminal sessions
- create/list/get/write/resize/kill
- output tail/clear
- command run helper
- bounded output buffer

`eliza.git`

- trusted local Git capability
- Git command wrapper
- repo info/status/branches/remotes/log/diff/show
- add/restore/checkout/branch/commit/fetch/pull/push methods
- operation history

`eliza.local-model`

- Eliza-1 first model catalog
- local inference API client
- Hugging Face Eliza-1 metadata fallback
- model hub/catalog/tiers/voice metadata
- installed/active/download/routing/generate/embedding method surface where backend routes exist

`eliza.runtime`

- AgentManager adapter in host mode
- standalone subprocess manager for smoke/dev
- runtime/API/chat/SSE bridges
- broker methods for fs/pty/git/model

### Build/package housekeeping

Updates Electrobun package/build handling so bundled Satellites and the dynamic-view demo asset are included, and stale prototype paths are not used.

Also adjusts macOS build icon handling so the committed `AppIcon.icns` path is used without depending on stale `.build` artifacts.

### Typecheck baseline repair

Fixes the Electrobun typecheck baseline by:

- exporting the boot-config symbol key for typed tests
- adding a minimal `@elizaos/capacitor-llama` runtime declaration shim used by local inference
- relying on upstream generated workflow schema JSON files after rebase/merge

## What This PR Does Not Do

- Does not add `eliza.voice`
- Does not add `eliza.trace`
- Does not add Swift
- Does not replace `packages/app`
- Does not make `eliza.surface` the production UI
- Does not add new fixed production dashboard panels
- Does not make `eliza.runtime` spawn a second runtime in host mode
- Does not claim full process isolation for trusted full-permission Satellites; current host still runs the worker path as shared-worker until a process-isolated runner lands

## Validation

Ran after merging current `origin/develop` into the WIP branch:

- `bun run --cwd packages/electrobun-carrots typecheck`
- `bun run --cwd packages/electrobun-carrots test`
- `bun run --cwd packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun typecheck`
- `bun run --cwd packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun test`
- `bun run --cwd packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun build`
- `git diff --check`

The Electrobun test suite now includes the dynamic view registry/session tests and worker host-request dispatch coverage.

Satellite validations run during this WIP branch:

- `bun run --cwd packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun/satellites/surface typecheck`
- `bun run --cwd packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun/satellites/surface build`
- `bun run --cwd packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun/satellites/surface smoke`
- Runtime Satellite build and Phase 1/2/3 smokes using safe standalone command mode
- FS typecheck/build/smoke
- PTY typecheck/build/smoke
- Git typecheck/build/smoke
- Local Model typecheck/build/smoke
- App icon presence in the built Electrobun bundle
- dynamic-view demo asset presence in the built Electrobun bundle
- no leftover smoke/dev processes after cleanup

Build notes:

- `bun install --ignore-scripts` tempdir permission errors appeared during desktop build and were tolerated by the existing build script.
- Vite/Rolldown emitted existing `@electric-sql/pglite` direct `eval` and ineffective dynamic import warnings.

## Review Notes

This should be reviewed as a WIP platform convergence PR.

The main review surfaces are:

- first-party Satellite seeding and package inclusion
- host request path for AgentManager-backed runtime behavior
- `carrotInvokeWorker` and `carrotTailWorkerEvents` typed RPC surfaces
- bounded event buffering semantics
- dynamic view manifest/session lifecycle and canvas/A2UI reuse
- trusted worker host-request access to dynamic views
- Surface staying dev/admin only
- whether the first-party Satellite manifests/docs honestly communicate trusted capability boundaries and current shared-worker host behavior

The intended next phase is an agent-run/tool/subagent trace layer that opens dynamic views automatically, not another fixed dashboard panel.

topPRs.title: fix(os): polish elizaOS live demo
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## Summary

This keeps the elizaOS Live / USB-demo branch current with `develop` and hardens the parts that were breaking fresh installs, package-mode validation, mobile packaging, live CI validation, and the Linux live demo surface.

- Polishes the elizaOS Live visual path across greeter, GNOME shell/window list, startup/failure shell, and voice onboarding.
- Keeps the bundled elizaOS/Milady app as the auto-starting home surface while preserving normal GNOME/Tails desktop use.
- Keeps the broken voice pill path opt-in until the pill renderer has a production UI.
- Hardens the USB installer write flow, backend contracts, startup-shell fallback assets, package-mode UI/test wiring, and runtime packaging checks.
- Carries the current local-inference/native merge state, including the latest `elizaOS/llama.cpp` submodule tip used by this branch.
- Fixes latest `develop` merge fallout around duplicate package exports, the Capacitor llama patcher/patch conflict, voice diarizer/phone package conflicts, Android mobile ONNX sidecars, duplicate SMS gateway package scripts, lockfile drift, formatting drift, and the local-inference CI build path.
- Adds the missing remote-capability GitHub live artifact validation path so manual/nightly live runs download and validate both Cloud and provider evidence artifacts.

## Current Source Base

Updated 2026-05-19 with normal merges, not rebases.

- PR head: `d3eb80c11ebeb72739a9ad59cada31b8a682ed9f`
- Latest merged `origin/develop`: `d32b6a446185e77267f1e51005a1796de244b43b`
- `plugins/plugin-local-inference/native/llama.cpp`: `96dd1a8466c84bdd419faf3866425260623fb6b0`
- Branch was pushed normally; no force-push was used for this refresh.
- GitHub currently reports `mergeStateStatus: UNSTABLE` because checks are rerunning on the latest pushed head.

## Latest Local Validation

Fresh local validation after merging latest `develop` and resolving the local-inference build-path conflict to upstream's cleaner `bun run build.ts` implementation:

```bash
bun install --frozen-lockfile
bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-local-inference build
bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-local-inference format:check
git diff --check
```

Results:

- Frozen install passed; postinstall reported `patch-llama-cpp-capacitor patched=0 already-applied=1 repaired=0 failed=0`.
- `@elizaos/plugin-local-inference` build passed using `bun run build.ts`, avoiding the previous CI failure from a bare `tsup` binary.
- `@elizaos/plugin-local-inference` format check passed: 339 files checked, no fixes applied.
- PR delta whitespace check passed.

Additional validation from the immediately preceding merge head:

```bash
bun run build:core
```

Result: 38/38 build tasks passed, including `@elizaos/plugin-local-inference`.

Earlier validation retained from this PR after previous latest-`develop` merges:

```bash
bun run format:check
bun run test:remote-capabilities
bun run test:remote-capabilities:live-ci-audit
bun run test:remote-capabilities:live-ci-audit:self-test
bun run --cwd packages/agent build:mobile
node --test packages/app-core/scripts/run-mobile-build-policy.test.mjs
node --check packages/scripts/audit-capability-router-live-ci.ts
node --check packages/scripts/audit-capability-router-live-ci.self-test.ts
```

Results:

- Full workspace format check passed: 75 tasks successful.
- Remote capability suite passed: 9 files, 190 tests passed, 3 skipped.
- Live CI audit passed: 45 checks.
- Live CI audit self-test passed: 45 checks.
- Android mobile bundle builds after the OmniVoice/ONNX cleanup and generated CSS-string step; bundle output no longer requires ONNX sidecars.
- Mobile build policy node:test passed: 24 tests.
- Live CI audit scripts parse cleanly under `node --check`.

Earlier validation retained from this PR:

```bash
bunx vitest run --config vitest.config.ts \
  plugins/plugin-local-inference/__tests__/voice-speaker-diarizer.test.ts \
  plugins/plugin-local-inference/__tests__/voice-speaker-encoder.test.ts \
  plugins/plugin-local-inference/src/services/engine.voice.test.ts \
  plugins/plugin-local-inference/src/services/voice/whisper-cpp-asr.test.ts
bun run verify:cloud
bun run --cwd packages/os/usb-installer test
bun packages/agent/scripts/build-mobile-bundle.mjs --target=ios
bun --config=/tmp/bunfig-no-coverage.toml test \
  plugins/plugin-aosp-local-inference/__tests__/aosp-kokoro-tts-handler.test.ts \
  plugins/plugin-aosp-local-inference/__tests__/aosp-local-inference-bootstrap.test.ts \
  --reporter=dots
```

Results: USB installer validation passed locally on the current branch lineage: unit/integration tests (9 files, 76 pass, 1 opt-in virtual-device skip), typecheck, build, expanded Biome lint over src/tests/server/config, Playwright e2e (6 pass across desktop/mobile mocked browser flows), and the guarded Linux virtual USB proof (scsi_debug block device, sudo -n dd write, readback SHA-256, cleanup verified). Earlier voice/local-inference, cloud verification, mobile bundle, and AOSP OmniVoice/TTS checks remain documented here but were not rerun after the final merge.

Earlier artifact validation from this PR remains documented here, but it was not re-run after the final `develop` merge:

- Built ISO: `packages/os/linux/variants/milady-tails/out/binary.iso`
- Validated ISO SHA256: `3a26c4990a5dcc53c4a5f3dcf3e6c400a0df5a06aeb6cf37c5fb03c210dfb9f2`
- QEMU visual validation confirmed the elizaOS greeter, light branding, normal GNOME desktop, app auto-launch, white/blue onboarding, and no broken pill-loader takeover.
- `./scripts/static-smoke.sh`, `./scripts/security-smoke.sh`, and `./scripts/runtime-api-smoke.sh` were previously run for that artifact.

## Current GitHub Status

- PR is draft while CI re-runs on `d3eb80c11e`.
- The previous red `Server Tests` failure was reproduced locally and fixed by adding the missing GitHub live artifact validation workflow path.
- The later `tsup: command not found` CI failure was addressed by merging upstream's `plugin-local-inference` `build.ts` fix and validating the package build locally.
- Current checks are queued/running on the new head; skipped fork/security/manual gates are expected.

## Remaining Hardware/Product Gaps

Before making hardware/product claims, still needed:

- Repeat guarded physical USB flash/readback for a fresh ISO built from the current head. The Linux fake-media and scsi_debug virtual block-device E2Es prove the guarded server/backend write path safely, including real Linux dd/readback, but they are not a hardware boot substitute.
- Boot that USB on real hardware.
- Validate real USB Persistent Storage create/unlock/delete behavior.
- Validate privacy/direct networking behavior for app, renderer, embedded browser, OAuth, and external web surfaces.
- Production release still needs real update keyring, revocation metadata, downloader UX, rollback health policy, SBOM, provenance, and formal inherited Tails sudoers review.

topPRs.title: Merge develop: BSC wallet attach + accumulated develop work
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
## Summary

Promote `develop` (36 commits) to `main`. The user-visible win:

- **`feat(cloud): let OAuth users attach a wallet to buy crypto credits`** (`74d23ff6e4`) — fixes the /bsc dead-end where OAuth-signed-in users (Google / Discord / GitHub / Magic Link / Passkey) couldn't pay because `user.wallet_address` was unset and there was no path to set it. Adds `POST /api/users/me/wallet/attach` (SIWE-verified) and an "Verify your BSC wallet" card on /bsc that drives connect → sign → attach → invalidate before handing off to the existing purchase flow. 6 unit tests on the route + 3 Playwright e2e tests on /bsc.

Other commits in this batch: doc tidies, benchmark fixes (Cerebras max_tokens, JSON extraction for Qwen, xlam dataset trust flag), runtime EventType doc alignment.

## Test plan
- [ ] CI on develop is green at HEAD (`74d23ff6e4`).
- [ ] Cloud CF Deploy fires on main → production Pages deploy serves the new /bsc with the attach card for OAuth users.
- [ ] Smoke test live `https://elizacloud.ai/bsc` while signed in via Google: the page now shows "Verify your BSC wallet" instead of the dead Pay button.
- [ ] Connect a BSC wallet → sign → see toast "Wallet verified — you can pay now" → card swaps to the existing purchase UI → buy with BSC promo.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

topPRs.title: feat(mobile): add local voice and sandbox routing
topPRs.author: Dexploarer
topPRs.body:
# Relates to - Follows the architecture work from #7779. - Accounts for #7844: upstream is collapsing the old Satellite/Carrot vocabulary into remote-mode Plugins (`mode: "direct" | "remote"`). This branch accepts the landed remote-plugin naming and keeps the implementation compatible with that direction. # Background ## What does this PR do? This PR makes the iOS app and remote coding path usable from mobile while preserving the new remote-plugin architecture boundary. - Adds the iOS local voice/onboarding path around the full Bun/native local-inference lane. - Moves mobile onboarding toward a simpler cloud/sky visual treatment with fewer cards, smaller radii, and clearer voice/download state. - Uses Kokoro/mobile TTS for onboarding greeting instead of blocking first-run on OmniVoice warmup. - Keeps local voice asset readiness separate from engine readiness so the UI does not claim synth success before a real probe works. - Carries the remote coding/sandbox work so mobile can route coding sessions to Eliza Cloud or home/remote workers instead of trying to run Codex/Claude/OpenCode/PTYs inside the iOS App Store client. - Keeps `plugin-coding-tools` parity scoped: `ls` can route through `eliza.fs`; grep/glob remain plugin-owned until explicit search/glob semantics exist; edit remains plugin-owned until a first-class patch/edit capability exists. - Reconciles the branch with latest `origin/develop`, including the Babylon -> Feed rename and the upstream remote-plugin vocabulary changes. ## Why is this needed? The product goal is that a user can open Eliza on a phone and still use the agent to talk, set up local voice/model state, sign into cloud, and start real coding work from any device. iOS cannot safely or App-Store-compatibly run arbitrary PTYs, Codex/Claude/OpenCode agent runners, or native build tools inside the client. Those jobs need to route to an Eliza Cloud sandbox or a trusted home/remote worker, while the mobile app stays the controller and renderer. This PR also removes ambiguity around voice readiness. Having the files on disk is not the same as having a working native synth engine, and onboarding should not pretend otherwise. # Risks Medium. This touches mobile onboarding, iOS local voice, capability routing tests, and the remote-plugin merge boundary. The riskiest areas are native iOS voice/runtime behavior on physical devices and CI paths that moved under the recent `develop` rename wave. # Documentation changes needed? Docs are included where this branch already introduced or updated the remote coding/sandbox and mobile paths. No separate migration guide is required for app users in this PR. # Testing ## Local validation run after reconciling `origin/develop` - `bun run test:remote-capabilities:surface-audit` - `ELIZAOS_STATIC_SOURCE_ONLY=1 ./scripts/static-smoke.sh` from `packages/os/linux/variants/milady-tails` - `bun run --cwd packages/ui typecheck` - `bun run --cwd packages/app typecheck` - `bun run --cwd packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun typecheck` - `bun run --cwd packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun test` - `bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-capacitor-bridge typecheck` - `bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-coding-tools typecheck` - `bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-coding-tools test src/actions/ls.test.ts src/actions/bash.test.ts --reporter verbose` - `node --check packages/app-core/scripts/kernel-patches/cpu-simd-kernels.mjs` - `node --check packages/app-core/scripts/kernel-patches/cpu-polar-kernels.mjs` - `node --check packages/app-core/scripts/build-llama-cpp-dflash.mjs` - `node --check packages/app-core/scripts/run-mobile-build.mjs` - `node --check packages/test/cloud-e2e/src/fixtures/stack.ts` - `node --check packages/tui/src/utils.ts` - `git diff --check` - `git diff --cached --check` ## Reviewer start point Start with: 1. `packages/ui/src/components/onboarding/VoicePrefixGate.tsx` 2. `packages/ui/src/components/onboarding/VoicePrefixSteps.tsx` 3. `packages/app-core/scripts/build-llama-cpp-dflash.mjs` 4. `plugins/plugin-coding-tools/src/actions/ls.ts` 5. Remote sandbox/cloud/home-worker docs and routing files touched by this branch # Deploy notes CI is rerunning on the pushed merge commit. #7844 is still open and dirty; this PR preserves compatibility with its stated direction without depending on it landing first.

Greptile Summary

 This PR wires up the iOS local-voice onboarding path, migrates remote sandbox/coding routing from \"satellite/carrot\" to a \"remote-plugin\" vocabulary, and adds a direct `invokeWorker`/`tailWorkerEvents` API to the `RemotePluginHost`. The UI onboarding states are removed in favour of a refactored `VoicePrefixSteps` component, and `ls` in `plugin-coding-tools` gains a capability-router fast path so mobile can delegate filesystem listings to Eliza Cloud. - **Vocabulary rename** – `E2BSatelliteCapabilityRouterService` and >40 env-variable names renamed; URL settings keep backward-compat fallbacks. - **`RemotePluginHost` extended** – new `invokeWorker`, `tailWorkerEvents`, and ring-buffered event log; service singletons in `rpc-handlers.ts` moved to module-level. - **`ls` routing** – capability-router fast path with local-FS fallback on `CAPABILITY_UNAVAILABLE`.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge; all findings are naming or type-strictness nits with no runtime impact. The core logic changes are all correct. The four flagged items are an env-variable name using old vocabulary, a redundant wire-type field, its mirror in the RPC schema, and a loosened TypeScript index-signature that could mask future mutation bugs but has no runtime consequence today. packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun/src/native/remote-plugin-host.ts and packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun/src/rpc-schema.ts carry the redundant satelliteId field and old-vocabulary env var.

Important Files Changed

 | Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun/src/native/remote-plugin-host.ts | Adds invokeWorker, tailWorkerEvents, ring-buffered event log, and three new host injection points; ELIZA_CARROT_MAX_WORKER_EVENTS uses old vocabulary and RemotePluginWorkerEventRecord carries a redundant satelliteId field. | | packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun/src/rpc-schema.ts | Adds RemotePluginWorkerEventRecord and RPC entries for dynamic views, trace, voice, database, and launch; satelliteId field is redundant with remotePluginId. | | packages/agent/src/services/e2b-capability-router.ts | Large mechanical rename from satellite/carrot to remote vocabulary; URL-based settings keep backward-compat fallbacks; logic unchanged. | | plugins/plugin-coding-tools/src/actions/ls.ts | Adds capability-router fast path; falls back to local FS on CAPABILITY_UNAVAILABLE; helper functions extracted cleanly. | | packages/ui/src/api/client-voice-profiles.ts | normaliseCaptureSession now returns a local fallback without server sessionId when prompts fail validation, correctly preventing mismatched audio uploads. | | packages/plugin-remote-manifest/src/types.ts | JsonObject index signature changed from readonly to mutable; new HostRequestMethod entries added. | | packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun/src/rpc-handlers.ts | VoiceService and LaunchOrchestrator moved to module-level singletons; large set of new RPC handlers wired up. |

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `packages/ui/src/components/onboarding/VoicePrefixGate.tsx`, line 523-537 ([link](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/blob/a3c58597f4fa5ab5a11d3acce066ffea612c2a7c/packages/ui/src/components/onboarding/VoicePrefixGate.tsx#L523-L537)) **useEffect dependency declared but never called inside the effect** `refreshVoiceBundleReadiness` is listed in the dependency array, but the effect body never invokes it — it defines its own inner `refresh` closure that calls `loadVoiceBundleReadiness` directly. Because `refreshVoiceBundleReadiness` is stable (empty `useCallback` deps), this doesn't cause extra re-runs, but the dependency is misleading and the `alive` guard inside the effect duplicates logic already present in `refreshVoiceBundleReadiness`.
Reviews (4): Last reviewed commit: ["fix: update capability naming audit self..."](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/4c6966c0e505173d6f8c92159068bbb86127667b) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=33064449)
